new_classes.label_en: Other Still Wine of Fresh Grapes (no PDO/PGI) in containers > 2 L with actual alcoholic strength > 15% vol (CN 2204 29 90)
new_classes.label_de: Anderer Stillwein aus frischen Weintrauben (ohne g.U./g.g.A.) in Behältnissen > 2 L mit einem vorhandenen Alkoholgehalt > 15 % vol (KN 2204 29 90)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes without Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) or Protected Geographical Indication (PGI), presented in containers with a volume greater than 2 litres, with an actual alcoholic strength by volume greater than 15% vol, classified under CN code 2204 29 90. The total dry extract of such wines, composed of non-volatile substances including sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colouring matter and salts, is relevant for classification per Additional Notes 4 and 5 to Chapter 22.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the main distinguishing criteria for CN 2204 29 90: (1) wine of fresh grapes as parent class, (2) large container (> 2 L) via the existing LargeContainer TBox class, (3) absence of PDO and PGI via complement restrictions, (4) actual alcoholic strength > 15% vol via a decimalRange restriction on alcoholByVolumePercent, and (5) relevance of total dry extract quality (linking to TotalDryExtractQuality already in the TBox). The legal note references Additional Notes 4 and 5 to Chapter 22, which define total dry extract as a sum of non-volatile substances (sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colouring matter, salts). The specific composition criteria of the total dry extract (enumerated substance types) cannot be fully formalised in OWL-DL without additional classes for each substance type; this is a known gap. The complement restrictions on PDO/PGI qualities are expressed as someValuesFrom complements, which is an approximation — full closed-world negation is not expressible in OWL-DL. The note also implicitly excludes semi-sparkling wine (excess pressure ≥ 1 bar) and sparkling wine (excess pressure ≥ 3 bar), which is not explicitly stated in the given legal text fragment but is implied by context; this exclusion is not formalised here. Coverage score is set to 0.75 to reflect these gaps.
